title: viola × albovii
intro: Viola × albovii is a hybrid species of the genus Viola, commonly known as the wild pansy or heartsease. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola × albovii include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as an antioxidant. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by drying and processing the leaves, flowers, and roots of the plant.
